implementation_drift.description: I ran the broader problem-space web_searches (queries 1-2) before formally writing 03_papers.json and 04/05. The output is consistent — those queries are reused as step 03 evidence and the candidate-specific queries 3-4 are recorded as step 06. But the order deviated. Logged here honestly.

domain_intelligence.description: Strong impulse to override the 2 forced hits on pure legal-source pages (trans-lex, legalexperts.ai) since they aren't LLM prior art. Did not override. They are noise; verdict is driven by AgentArmor and Graph-Augmented Agents Survey agent-judged hits.

honest_note: Strong feeling that AgentArmor (2508.01249) is the closest substantive prior art and the estoppel framing was a relabeling. Specifically: AgentArmor's Program Dependence Graph over agent runtime IS the mechanism I was proposing for sub-problems 1 and 2 (reliance tracking + lock-on-rely). The only contribution my candidate would add over AgentArmor is the normative framing (locking discipline + cascade-recovery protocol with estoppel name). Per program §10 — that's not a paradigm shift, that's a renaming with adjacent protocol details. Verdict FAIL is honest. Two pure-legal-doctrine forced hits are source-domain noise but consistent with the round 002 pattern where source-domain papers mechanically match domain-specific content_words. Net: candidate fails on substantive grounds (AgentArmor-line), independent of the source-domain noise.
